title: Journal of General Virology
bodyHtml:
JGV Editorial Board 2014

ID numbers are from Bench>Press. Click here for contact details of JGV Editors.

A. Antonsson (Australia), ID 27980
Skin papillomaviruses, animal papillomaviruses,
polymerase chain reaction, phylogenetic analysis
M. A. Aranda (Spain), ID 34522
Plant RNA viruses, genetic resistance to plant viruses
C. R. M. Bangham (UK), ID 25788
Immune response to viruses (especially cellular
immunity) and immune evasion; evolution and dynamics
of virus infections; CNS virus infections; retroviruses
(immunology, dynamics)
J. N. Barr (UK), ID 23622
Negative strand RNA virus transcription
A. D. T. Barrett (USA), ID 25789
Flaviviruses, alphaviruses, bunyaviruses,
arthropod-borne viruses
E. Bejarano (Spain), ID 33935
Plant DNA viruses, plant–virus interaction, insect–virus
interaction, postranslational modifications by ubiquitin
and ubiquitin-like molecules, hormone responses in plants, gene silencing in plants
G. Belsham (Denmark), ID 23644
Molecular biology of picornaviruses, especially foot-and-mouth disease viruses, pestiviruses
B. Berkhout (Netherlands), ID 25791
Retroviruses, HIV, molecular evolution,
antiviral therapy, drug resistance
H.-U. Bernard (USA), ID 31004
Transcription of papillomaviruses, epigenetics of papillomaviruses,
evolution of papillomaviruses, taxonomy of papillomaviruses,
epidemiology of papillovaviruses and associated cancers
K. Bernard (USA), ID 35154
Viral pathogenesis, host response and transmission of arboviruses, with a focus on flaviviruses and alphaviruses
D. J. Blackbourn (UK), ID 25792
Herpesviruses, especially KSHV/HHV-8, including
immune modulation by them
C. Crump (UK), ID 26116
Herpesviruses and polyomaviruses
A. D. Davidson (UK), ID 31776
Flaviviruses, arthropod-borne viruses, positive-sense RNA viruses, virus/host interactions
A. J. Davison (UK), ID 23880
Herpesvirus (especially genomics)
P. Digard (UK), ID 23916
Influenza viruses, RNA polymerases, negative strand viruses, antivirals
J. Doorbar (UK), ID 27026 
Molecular biology of human papillomavirus infection; in particular,
events that regulate normal productive infection in the skin; how these events become deregulated during cancer progression
D. Durantel (France), ID 23941
Hepatitis B virus, HBV innate immunity, immune evasion
G. Elliott (UK), ID 26179
Herpesvirus assembly and egress, herpesvirus tegument proteins, nucleo-cytoplasmic trafficking of viral proteins
R. M. Elliott (UK), ID 25793
Bunyaviruses, negative strand viruses, RNA viruses, molecular biology, virus host interactions
 
 
 
 
D. J. Evans (UK), ID 25794
Picorna viruses, virus receptors, positive-sense RNA viruses
K. Faaberg (USA), ID 27009
Arterivirus replication, infectious clones, construction of novel vaccines, viral genomics and molecular
pathogenicity (especially porcine reproductive and respiratory disease virus)
P. J. Farrell (UK), ID 28146
Epstein-Barr virus, KSHV, HHV8, herpesvirus cancer biology, tumour virology
A. R. Fooks (UK), ID 24019
Zoonoses, RNA viruses, vector-borne viruses, exotic viruses
D. Gatherer (UK), ID 40344
Virus evolution, bioinformatics, systems biology and viruses, the role of viruses in history (e.g. pandemics)
A. Gill (UK), ID 34614
Prion diseases and other protein misfolding diseases; protein chemistry-related disciplines including protein expression, purification, characterisation and analysis;
characterisation of viral proteomes or the effect that viruses have on the proteonome
L. Gillet (Belgium), ID 36250
Gammaherpesviruses, veterinary viruses
S. M. Gray (USA), ID 33277
Plant viruses, insect transmission of viruses, host resistance to viruses, virus epidemiology, virus disease control
S. Griffin (UK), ID 25955
Hepatitis C virus, HCV and liver cancer, virus ion channels, antivirals, oncolytic viruses, + strand RNA viruses (animal),  viruses and cancer, virus entry
M. R. Hajimorad (USA), ID 27280
Plant RNA viruses, virus/host interactions, experimental evolution, virus gene characterization, adaptation
B. Hale (UK), ID 28369
Negative-sense RNA viruses (animal),  post-translational modifications, cell signalling, innate immunity, virus–host interaction
T. C. Harder (Germany), ID 24156
Negative-stranded viruses, influenza virus, morbillivirus, molecular epidemiology, molecular diagnostic tools
M. Harris (UK), ID 24160
Positive stranded RNA-viruses, retrovirus, hepatitis C virus, HIV
E. Herniou (France), ID 27720
Baculoviruses, evolution, phylogeny, ecology, coevolution, virus–host interaction, insect viruses
E. C. Holmes (Australia), ID 24204
Virus evolution, virus evolution, dengue, flavivirus, phylogeny, influenza
N. Hunter (UK), ID 25797
Prion
M. Jarvis (UK), ID 51985
Vaccine development, herpesviruses, haemorrhagic fever viruses
C. Jopling (UK), ID 27823
MicroRNA regulation, translation and other RNA mechanisms applied to viruses in general
A. A. Khromykh (Australia), ID 28484
Flavivirus replication, flavivirus–host interactions, interferon response, flavivirus vaccines, viral vectors
G. Kurath (USA), ID 33258
IHN virus, fish rhabdoviruses, fish paramyxoviruses, viral shedding
K. N. Leppard (UK), ID 29248
Adenoviruses, gene expression, cell nucleus, adenovirus vectors, virus–host interactions
C. McCormick (UK), ID 26194
Hepatitis C and related viruses, noroviruses
M. A. McCrae (UK), ID 27023
Rotaviruses  — molecular biology of dsRNA containing viruses, i.e.  reoviridae, Vif protein of HIV-1, various aspects of vaccine design and development
M. McGarvey (UK), ID 39168
Hepatitis C virus and related viruses, hepatitis B virus and other hepadnaviruses; areas related to these
A. Merits (Estonia), ID 24631
Positive-strand RNA viruses (especially alphaviruses and HCV), antivirals, host factors for virus replication, virus-encoded enzymes and proteins, virus replication and replication complexes
 
 J. Ortin (Spain), ID 26369
Influenza virus, RNA replication and transcription, polymerase, virus–host interactions, gene expression
P. F. Palukaitis (Republic of Korea), ID 24821
Plant responses to virus infection, plant-virus interactions, satellite RNAs of plant viruses, viral silencing suppressors and viroids
B. Paxton (UK), ID 32666
HIV-1 interactions, HIV-1 transmission,
HIV-1 and host factors, HIV-1 disease progression
S. Poehlmann (Germany), ID 28335
Influenza, corona and filoviruses; HIV; host cell entry; proteolytic activation; innate immunity
M. J. Roossinck (USA), ID 25798
Plant RNA viruses, fungal viruses, virus evolution
W. L. Schneider (USA), ID 25050
Virus evolution, plant virology (both plant RNA viruses and DNA viruses)
S. Schultz-Cherry (USA), ID 34298
Influenza virus, enteric viruses (including astrovirus, norovirus, calicivirus), viral pathogenesis,  viral immunology, vaccines and antiviral related to influenza or enteric viruses, cellular responses to viral infections, veterinary virology
M. Schwemmle (Germany), ID 26661
Influenza virus, borna disease virus, negative-strand RNA viruses, neurovirology, protein complexes, protein–protein Interactions, viral polymerase, viral replication
J. Sinclair (UK), ID 27081
Control of herpesvirus gene expression, viral transcriptional regulators, viral regulation of cell cycle and apoptosis
M. A. Skinner (UK), ID 28752
Avian poxviruses, recombinant vaccines, interferon
B. Slobedman (Australia), ID  34968
Herpesviruses (CMV, VZV), latent infection, viral immunity, viral immune evasion, dendritic cells, myeloid cells, neuronal infection
G. L. Smith (UK), ID 27895
Poxviruses
B. Sodeik (Germany), ID 25141
Herpesviruses, cell biology of viral infection, intracellular trafficking, cell biology of poxviruses
P. Staeheli (Germany), ID 25163
Negative-sense RNA viruses, interferons and innate immunity
J. Steel (USA), ID 35137
Influenza virus pathogenesis, transmission and vaccination
S. Stertz (Switzerland), ID 46457
Influenza virus entry, RNAi screens for host factors required for replication, antivirals for influenza, virus–host interactions, restriction factors for influenza viruses (Mx, IFITM etc.)
Y. Takeuchi (UK), ID 39804
Therapy vectorology,  viral receptors, xenotransplantation,  zoonosis
A. S. Turnell (UK), ID 28067
DNA viruses and virus–cell interactions
A. Vaira (Italy), ID 38120
Family ophioviridae, plant ambisense/negative strand RNA virus, plant–virus interaction, plant virus infectious clones
M. van Oers (Netherlands), ID 25330
Insect viruses including dicistroviruses, iflaviruses, iridoviruses, baculoviruses, nudiviruses, hytrosaviruses, WSSV
S. C. Weaver (USA), ID 27039
Zoonotic virus epidemiology, evolution and ecology; alphavirus pathogenesis; vaccine development; virus–arthropod vector interactions
C. Wobus (USA), ID 30237
+ sense RNA viruses, enteric viruses, calciviruses, virus entry, viral receptors, pathogenesis
R. Zell (Germany), ID 30516
Influenza viruses, picornavruses, varicella zoster virus, herpes simplex virus
X. Zhou (PR China), ID 28670
Plant DNA viruses, viruses infecting rice, virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS), transgenic research for virus resistance

 

 

Last modified 1 January 2014
